monitored_query: How much does it cost to build a car wash in Texas?
base_value_excerpt: If you are evaluating the cost to build a car wash in Texas, 2026 is a strong but capital-intensive market to enter. Commercial construction costs rose roughly 6% year-over-year heading into 2026 (Gordian Q1 2025 Construction Cost Report), and car wash development sits at the higher-complexity end of commercial construction because it combines a building, heavy equipment, water systems, and an environmentally regulated site.
answer_block:
Building a car wash in Texas in 2026 costs between $1.2 million and $8 million or more, depending on facility type. A self-serve (3–5 bay) site runs $1.2M–$2.5M all-in; an in-bay automatic runs $1.2M–$2.8M; an express exterior tunnel runs $3.0M–$7.0M; and a full-service or flex-service site runs $4.0M–$8.0M+. The building shell costs $250–$400 per sq ft excluding land (Gordian Q1 2025; RSMeans 2025). Land, facility type, and Texas metro are the three biggest cost variables.

  • Self-serve (3–5 bays): $1.2M–$2.5M
  • In-bay automatic: $1.2M–$2.8M
  • Express exterior tunnel: $3.0M–$7.0M
  • Full-service / flex-service: $4.0M–$8.0M+
